the university of southern california was founded in 1879 and was formally opened for students october, 1880. it is under control of a board of directors, elected annually by the southern california conference of the methodist episcopal church. the endowment fund is under the control and management of separate boards of trustees. the past year has been a steady gain over the year previous. education within the walls of the school, while positively christian, is in no wise sectarian. the textbooks in use are selected wholly on account of their merits, and not for denominational purposes or ends. the institution will treat the most advanced views on all subjects, its aims being symmetry of life and character. it believes in a richly endowed humanity. as early as 1874 it became apparent that los angeles was to become one of the great centers of the pacific coast. a variety of circumstances contributed to make this an irresistible conclusion. railroads from the north and east were working their way, slowly but surely, toward the southern coast. the wide and beautiful valley of los angeles, healthful and fertile from one end to the other, with its unsurpassed climate and charming scenery, combined to make this one of the most favorite spots on the continent. john r. tausey, presiding bakic

of Dispensary Clinics; A. F. Darling, M. D., Professor of of Ophthalmology and Otology; R. M. Widney, attorney at law, Professor of Medical Jurisprudence; W. Edgar Lindley, M. D., Demonstrator of Anatomy.

CLINICAL FACILITIES-The College has the use, for clinical purposes, of the City and County Hospital, containing one hundred and fifty beds, and of the Los Angeles Infirmary, under charge of the Sisters of Charity, having one hundred and fifty beds. The new Infirmary building, which has just been finished at a cost of about $80,000, is in its appointments one of the most complete in the United States. In these hospitals may be found, owing to the cosmopolitan character of the population, and the constant travel by sea and by land from all parts of the world, disease in all its forms and in almost every race and nationality. Clinical lectures, both surgical and medical, are delivered regularly to the classes in both institutions, and the students are given every opportunity to examine the cases.

A free dispensary is also established at the College building, with an average daily attendance of from twenty to twenty-five sick. Daily clinics are held under the Chief of the Dispensary Clinics, and the various professors. Students are enabled to witness daily a wide range of cases in surgery, medicine, gynecology, ophthalmology and otology, venerial diseases, and diseases of children. Advanced students will be put in charge of cases under the supervision of the Chief of Clinics.

All medicines for the free dispensary are compounded in the College building, under the observation of, and largely by, the medical students, so that a practical course in pharmacy is added to their other training.

Arrangements are made by which the numerous autopsies of the Coroner's office are utilized when deemed advantageous for purposes of instruction in morbid, traumatic and surgical anatomy, and medical jurisprudence.

Arrangements for dissecting are very complete, and the supply of material abundant. As in the other Colleges of the University, both sexes are admitted to the course. A separate dissecting room is, however, furnished for women students.

CALEDONIA CLUB-Meets first and third Thursdays in each month, at No. 17 West First street, second floor. J. O. McLain, Chief; A. S. McDonald, Treasurer; R. Hardy, Secretary; Gregor McDonald, Piper.

CARPENTERS' HALL ASSOCIATION-Incorporated April 15, 1887, under the auspices of the Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners of America Union, No. 56. C. G. Willman, President; J. Wesley Wilson, Secretary.
TURN VEREIN GERMANIA-Meets each Wednesday evening.

LOS ANGELES COUNTY MEDICAL SOCIETY-Organized and Incorporated January 31st, 1871. Meets at the office of the President on first Friday of each month, at 7.30 P. M. Annual elections first Tuesday in January. Purpose: cultivation and advancement of the science of medicine, and promotion of the interests of the fraternity of the regular profession in this county. Dr. Fred. Bicknell, President; Dr. M. Hagan, Vice-President; Dr. Granville McGowan, Secretary; Dr. E. T. Schumacher, Treasurer. Office of President, 1-2 Schumacher Block.

LOS ANGELES HUMANE SOCIETY-Directors, H. Sinsabaugh, E. L. Baxter, C. C. Davis, Katherine Hooker, E. W. Jones, C. L. Wills, H. T. Lee, J. G. Leal, S. Woodhead, M. D. Lummis, H. L. Baker. Officers-H. T. Lee, President; Dorothea Lummis, Secretary; M. V. Wright, agent, office rear of B. J. Taney's Court, 75 Temple Block. Objects: The prevention of cruelty to children and animals, and the punishment of offenders against the laws made for their protection. The chief aim of the society is to create and foster such a public sentiment as shall render such offenses rare. To this end the hearty co-operation of all good citizens is sought. Under our laws the work is done under two organizations, one for the prevention of cruelty to children, and the other for the prevention of cruelty to animals. The annual dues in each society are $2.50. Life membership in the S. P. C. A., $50, in the S. P. C. C., contributing members, $1 per year; junior members (children under 15 years), fifty cents; honorary life members, $100.

LOS ANGELES ORPHANS' HOME SOCIETY-Location, northwest corner of Virgin and Yale streets. Organized July, 1881. Average number of orphans being cared for, 70. The object of this society shall be to take under its care orphan, half-orphan and destitute children, and provide them with a home, sustenance and education. Any person may be an active member of this society by the annual payment of two dollars; an honorary member by the annual payment of ten dollars; and a life member by the payment of one hundred dollars at one time. Mrs. D. G. Stephens, President; Mrs. M. A. Gibson, Vice-President; Mrs. S. R. DePeuy, Recording Secretary; R. H. Hewitt, Financial Secretary; Mrs. A. S. Averill, Corresponding Secretary; B. F. Coulter, Treasurer.

LADIES' BENEVOLENT SOCIETY-Meets every third Wednesday in each month at Pythian Castle, 24 South Spring street. Mrs. C. Ducommun, President; Mrs. B. Chandler, First Vice-President; Mrs. H. L. Bath, Second Vice-President; Mrs. C. J. Wollweber, Secretary; Mrs. I. W. Hellman, Treasurer.

BRITISH BENEVOLENT SOCIETY-Meets every Monday in each month at University Bank Building. A. J. Hutchinson, President; G. G. Charlton, First Vice-President; James Taylor, Second Vice-President; W. R. Blackman, Hon. Secretary: H. L. Macniel, Hon. Treasurer.

ITALIAN BENEVOLENT SOCIETY-Meets second Sunday in each month at 108 North Los Angeles street. Organized July, 1877. A. Sinocchio, President; G. Solvati, Vice-President; G. Castruccio, Treasurer; R. C. Devoto, Secretary.

ITALIAN UNION AND FRATERNAL SOCIETY-Meets first Sunday in each month, at 24 Olivera street. Giacomo Tononi, President; Ambrogio Vignolo, First Vice-President; Ferdinando Ricchini, Second Vice-President; Charles B. Pironi, Treasurer; Filippo Slatro, Secretary.
INDEPENDENT ORDER OF GOOD TEMPLARS-Merrill Lodge meets every Saturday evening at Pythian Hall, 24 South Spring street.
SONS OF TEMPERANCE-Los Angeles Division, No. 344-Organized February, 1886-Meets every Saturday evening, at Good Templars' Hall. W. B. Wadman, P. G. W. P., State Deputy.

ROYAL TEMPLARS OF TEMPERANCE-Los Angeles Council, No. 1-Organized 1884. Meets each Wednesday evening, at 451 North Spring street. George E. Howe, S. C.; Dr. W. N. Davis, Recording Secretary; J. P. Lamoree, Financial Secretary.
LADIES' AID SOCIETY-Meets at First Congregational Church, corner of Hill and Third streets, second Tuesday in each month.
LADIES' MISSIONARY SOCIETY-Meets at First Congregational Church, corner of Hill and Third streets, first Tuesday in each month.

organization_name: YOUNG PEOPLE'S SOCIETY OF CHRISTIAN ENDEAVOR
line: YOUNG PEOPLE'S SOCIETY OF CHRISTIAN ENDEAVOR-Meets at First Congregational Church, corner of Hill and Third streets, each Sunday evening at 6 o'clock.
AKRON BAND LITTLE MISSIONARY SOCIETY-Meets at First Congregational Church, corner of Hill and Third streets, every Saturday afternoon at 2:30 o'clock.

YOUNG MEN'S CHRISTIAN ASSOCIATION-Location, 212 West Second street. Membership, 175. The rooms are neat and commodious, and open to visitors from 8:30 A. M. to 9:30 P. M. About 300 volumes are on hand for the perusal of visitors, with a great many daily and weekly newspapers on file. Lyman Stewart, President; E. S. Field, First Vice-President; H. W. Mills, second Vice-President; A. H. Voigt, Treasurer; B. W. Diehl, Recording Secretary; W. H. Mead, General Secretary.

YOUNG MEN'S INSTITUTE-Branch No. 14-Membership, 250. Qualifications for membership, Catholic faith and good moral character. Objects, literary improvement. Meets every first and third Monday of each month, in hall of Church of Our Lady of Los Angeles, North Main street, near Plaza Square.

Boyle Heights Methodist Episcopal Church. Location, corner of St. Louis street and Pennsylvania Avenue. Rev. F. W. Johnson, Pastor; residence adjoining church. Services are held every Sunday at 11 a. M. and 7:30 P. M. Prayer meeting every Thursday at 7:30 г. м. Class meeting each Sunday at 12:15 P. M. Sunday School at 3 P. M.

Fort Street Methodist Episcopal Church. Location Fort, between Third and Fourth streets. Rev. R. S. Cantine, Pastor; residence 425 South Fort. Services Sundays at 10:45 A. M. and 7:80 P. M. Prayer meetings Thursdays at 7:30 P. M. Holiness meeting Sundays at 8 P. M. Sunday school at 12:30 P. M. Young People's meeting Mondays at 7:80 P. M. Teacher's meeting Wednesdays at 7:80 г. м.

German Methodist Episcopal Church. Location, Fourth street, between Hill and Fort. Rev. L. E. Schneider, Pastor; residence, 246 South Hill street. Services Sundays at 10:45 A. M. and 7:80 P. M. Prayer meetings Wednesdays at 7:30 P. M. Singing school Thursdays at 7:30 P. M. Bible class Friday at 7:30 г. м. Sunday school at 9:30 a. M. Young people's meeting Sundays at 6:30 P. M. All services in the German language.

Grace Methodist Episcopal Church. Location 445 East First street. Rev. William A. Knighten, Pastor; residence 27 Vine. Sunday services at 11 a. M. and 7:30 P. M. Sunday school at 2:15 P. M. Prayer meetings Wednesday evenings.

Main Street Methodist Episcopal Church. Location, corner Main and Laurel streets. Rev. I. L. Spencer, Pastor; residence, 24 Morris street. Services Sundays at 11 A. M. and 7:80 P. M. Young people's meeting Tuesdays at 7:30 P. M. Prayer meetings Thursdays at 7:30 P. M. Sunday school at 2:30 г. м.

Trinity Methodist Episcopal Church (South). Location Fort, bet Fifth and Sixth streets. Rev. W. B. Stradley, Pastor; residence 1245 South May. Services are held every Sunday at 11 a. M. and 7:30 P. M. Sunday school at 9:80 л. м.

University Methodist Episcopal Church. University Tract, corner Wesley avenue and Simpson street. Rev. F. B. Cherington, Pastor; residence, corner Hellman street and McClintock avenue. Services Sundays at 11 a. M. and 7:30 P. M. Sunday school at 9 4. M. Prayer meetings Thursdays at 7:30 P. M. Young people's meeting Sundays at 6:30 P. M.

First Presbyterian Church. Location, corner Second and Fort streets. Rev. William J. Chichester, Pastor; residence, 752 Pearl street. Services Sundays at 11 A. M. and 7:30 P. M. Prayer meetings Thursdays at 7:45 P. M. Young people's meeting Sundays at 6:80 p.m.

Sunday school at 9:30 A. M. Young People's meeting Mondays at 7:30 P. M. Prayer meeting Wednesdays at 7:30 P. M. This Church has a comfortable audience room, seating 400 persons, conveniently located on center of Downey avenue. Number of communicants, 75.

Third Presbyterian Church. Location, corner Hill and Pine streets. Rev. W. C. Stevens, Pastor; residence, corner Santee and Celis streets. Services on Sunday at 11 A. M. and 7:30 P. M. Sunday school at 9:80 A. M. Young people's meeting Sundays at 6:15 P. M. Prayer meeting on Thursdays at 7:30 P. M.

Presbyterian Mission, United Chinese. Location, 40 Wilmington. Organized, 1878. In charge of J. C. Nevin; residence 630 South Fort street.

Hebrew-Jewish Synagogue. Location, 114-116 South Fort street. Rev. Dr. E. D. D. Schreiber, rabbi; residence, Grand avenue, between Sixth and Seventh streets. Services are held each Friday evening, and each Saturday morning at 10 o'clock. Organized 1872.

Hartnett, Secretary. Rev. C. Scannel, Assistant. Pastoral residence, corner South Main and Second street. Masses Sun- days at 6, 8 and 10 o'clock A. Μ. Week days at 6 and 8 o'clock A. M. Vespers Sunday evenings at 7 o'clock.

organization_name: Church of Our Lady of Los Angeles
line: Church of Our Lady of Los Angeles. Location, Main street, opposite the Plaza. Rev. Peter Verdaguer, Rector; Rev. L. Gandon and Rev. B. Smythe, Assistants. Pastoral residence, adjoining church. Masses Sunday at 6, 8, 9 and 10:80 o'clock A. Μ. Sermon in English at 8 4. M. Sermon in Spanish at 10:30 A. M. Vespers Sunday at 3 P. M. Sunday school at 2 P. M. Devotional services at 6, 6:80 and 7 o'clock A. M., week days.

Mrs. Bengough's Private School-Location, 145 South Main, Mrs. Bengough, teacher. Organized in 1877. Average number of scholars, 35.

Woodbury's Business College. Location, Woollacott Block. This college was established in July, 1884, by F. C. Woodbury, formerly one of the proprietors of Heald's Business College of San Francisco. It is in a flourishing condition and ranks among the leading schools of Southern California. Principal, F. W. Kelsey; assistants, Miss Nannie Tibbetts and A. Cuiaz.

Lawson's Commercial Night School.. Location, rooms 11 and 12 Schumacher Block. This school was established by Mr. L. B. Lawson in January, 1886, for the purpose of affording an opportunity to those engaged during the day to obtain by evening study a knowledge of accounts and other branches essential to a business education. MASONIC BOARD OF RELIEF-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the Masters and a Committee of three of the four Lodges holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said Lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the Board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized in 1885. William H. White, Secretary, 211 New High.

line: THE GRAND CHAPTER OF THE ORDER OF THE EASTERN STAR OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA-Meets in the City of San Francisco (unless otherwise ordered) on the Monday following the second Tuesday of October in each year. The Grand Chapter was organized April 7, 1873. The first subordinate Chapter was organized May 9, 1869. There are now seventy-six subordinate Chapters under the jurisdiction of the Grand Chapter, of which one is located in the City of Los Angeles, viz: ACACIA CHAPTER No. 21-Meets first and third Saturday of each month at Masonic Temple.

EAST SIDE BANK-Incorporated November, 1886. Capital, $100,000. Number of shares of stock issued, 1,000 of $100 each. Directors: Thomas Meredith, S. C. Newton, F. E. Washburn, Wm. J. Washburn, L. Snodgrass and J. F. Towell.

organization_name: FARMERS' AND MERCHANTS' BANK OF LOS ANGELES
line: FARMERS' AND MERCHANTS' BANK OF LOS ANGELES-Established in 1871. Capital stock, $500,000, fully paid up. Surplus and profits, $643,000. Directors: Isaias W. Hellman, C. E. Thom, J. B. Lankershim, C. Ducommun, L. L. Bradbury, Emeline Childs, H. W. Hellman, L. C. Goodwin and Andrew Glassell.

FIRST NATIONAL BANK-Incorporated, 1880. Capital stock, $200,000. Number of shares of stock issued, 2,000. Amount paid in, $200,000. Surplus $235,000. Directors: J. D. Bicknell, J. F. Crank, H. Mabury, E. F. Spence, William Lacy, S. H. Mott and J. M. Elliott.

LOS ANGELES COUNTY BANK-Incorporated 1874. Capital stock, $100,000, fully paid up. Reserve fund, $100,000. Number of shares of stock issued, 1,000. Directors: S. B. Dewey, John E. Plater, Llewellyn Bixby, J. Bixby, Robert S. Baker, George W. Prescott and George H. Stewart.

LOS ANGELES NATIONAL BANK-Incorporated June, 1883. Capital stock, $500,000. Number of shares of stock issued, 5,000. Surplus, $80,000. Directors: Dr. W. G. Cochran, Gov. H. H. Markham, Perry M. Green, Dr. H. Sinsabaugh, F. C. Howes, John Bryson, George H. Bonebrake, and Warren Gillelen.

NATIONAL BANK OF CALIFORNIA-Established September 2, 1889. Capital, $250,000. Directors: Owen H. Churchill, Thomas R. Bard, M. H. Sherman, W. L. Graves, O. T. Johnson, E. F. C. Klokke, Dan McFarland, Fred. Eaton, Perry Wildman, W. G. Hughes, J. M. C. Marble, A. Hadley and John Wolfskill.
SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA NATIONAL BANK-Incorporated July, 1886.

Pressmen's Union, No. 29-First Tuesday in each month at 322 South Main street. Henry M. Bruning, Secretary, 124 West Twelfth street.

North Main street. J. K. Hamill, Secretary, Trades Hall, SE cor East First and Los Angeles streets.

line: United Order of Railway Employes, B. of R. T. (B. of L. F., S. M. A. A. and B. of R. C.) Meets first Tuesday of each month at B. of L. F. Hall, corner New Main and Leroy. A. W. Shuster, secretary.
Ancient Order of Hibernians. Meets at Hibernia Hall, 115 North Main street, at 2 P. M. the first Sunday of each month.

organization_name: Benevolent and Protective Order of Elks
line: Benevolent and Protective Order of Elks. LOS ANGELES LODGE, No. 99-Meets at Elks' Hall, 254 South Main street, every Sunday at 8 P. M. А. Е. Elser, Secretary.

Catholic Knights of America. ST. JOSEPH BRANCH, No. 397-Meets first and third Thursdays of each month, in hall at Church of Our Lady of Los Angeles, North Main street near Plaza Square. John P. Moran, President; John Mesmer, Vice-President; A. P. Anderson, Recording Secretary; G. LeSage, Financial Secretary; J. J. Rodriguez, Treasurer.

organization_name: United Order of the Golden Cross
line: United Order of the Golden Cross. ORANGE BRANCH COMMANDERY, No. 306-Meets every Friday evening, at 8 o'clock, Hayden's Hall, 5104 Downey avenue, East Los Angeles. Organized January 26, 1887. Membership, twenty-five. Objects: To unite fraternally, and to give moral and material aid to its members; also to establish a benefit fund for sick and distressed members.
Knights of the Golden Eagle. Meet at Elks' Hall, 254 South Main street, every Wednesday evening at 8 P. M.
The Modern Woodsmen. Meet at Elks' Hall, 254 South Main street, every Thursday evening at 8 P. M.
Independent Order of Good Templars. MERRILL LODGE-Meets every Monday evening at Temperance Temple, corner Temple and Broadway streets.
Order Sons of Herman. No. 12 Meets every Monday evening at 8 P. M., at Turn Verein Hall. Frank Langer, Secretary.
Knights and Ladies of Honor. Meet every Monday evening at Y. M. I. Hall, No. 107 North Main street.

Young Mens' Institute. BRANCH No. 14-Membership, 200. Qualifications for membership, Catholic faith and good moral character. Object, literary improvement. Meets first and third Tuesday of each month, at 8:30 P. M., in Y. M. I. Hall, 115 North Main street.
Knights of Robert Emmet.
Miscellaneous Societies.

Bar Association of Los Angeles-Organized June 5, 1888. Meets every first Saturday of each month at the Law Library. Officers: J. D. Bicknell, President; A. Brunson, First Vice-President; J. R. Finlayson, Second Vice-President; R. N. Bulla, Treasurer; J. W. Swannvick, Recording Secretary; J. A. Anderson Jr., Corresponding Secretary.

organization_name: Board of Trade of Los Angeles
line: Board of Trade of Los Angeles-Organized March 14, 1883. Number of members, 168. Office, northwest corner of Broadway and West First street. Objects: For the equal protection of the interests of its members; to prevent settlements without full investigation; the attachment by any member of this Board of any common debtor or debtors shall be for the mutual benefit of all Board members having claims against said debtor or debtors. Officers: Geo. E. Dickson, President; Carl Seligman, Vice-President; Gregory Perkins Jr., Secretary; L. N. Breed, Treasurer.
British Benevolent Society-Meets first Monday in each month at Lanfranco Building. William Lacy, President; Thomas Pascoe, Vice-President; Edward Malim, Secretary and Treasurer.

Caledonian Club-Meets first and third Tuesday of each month at Caledonian Hall, 1194 South Spring street, second floor. John Spiers, Chief; John Grant, Chieftain; Jas. Smith, Treasurer; Thomas Davidson, M. D., Secretary; Gregor McDonald, piper.
California Club-Organized 1888. Location, northwest corner Broadway and First street. Officers, W. G. Hughes, President; C. Cabot, Vice-President; J. W. Montgomery, Secretary.

organization_name: Chamber of Commerce of Los Angeles
line: Chamber of Commerce of Los Angeles-Organized October 15, 1888. Present membership, 450. Location, Main street bet First and Second. Meetings, second Wednesday of each month at 7:30 P. M. Its objects are to foster and encourage commerce; to stimulate home manufactures; to induce immigration and the subdivision, settlement and cultivation of our lands; to assist in the development of the material resources of the region, and generally to promote the business interests of Los Angeles City and County and the country tributary thereto. C. M. Wells, President; E. Germain, First Vice-President; J. R. Mathews, Second Vice-President; J. B. Lankershim, Third Vice-President; L. N. Breed, Treasurer; H. Jay Hanchette, Secretary.
Chautauqua Literary Circle-Meets every Tuesday evening, at Harvard Military Academy, St. Vincent Building.
Cremation Society of Southern California-Location, SW corner of Sixth and Broadway streets. H. Sinsabaugh, President; G. A. Dobinson, Treasurer, W. LeMoyne Wills, M. D., Secretary.
Dania Society-Meets every Thursday evening, San Pedro near Second streets. J. C. Nelson, President; P. Lanson, Secretary.
Divine Healing Association-Meets every Thursday at 10:30 A. M. at Temperance Temple, NW corner Temple and Broadway streets.

Ellis Club--Organized April 3, 1888, for the study of music for male voices, and also for the culture and development of a refined musical taste in its members. Active members (singing), 60; auxiliary members (substitutes), 12; associate members (subscribing), 200. Supported by an annual subscription of $10 from the associate members. Gives four concerts each year, to each of which the associate members receive four tickets. It is a strictly private organization, the membership being elective, and no tickets sold. Does not take part in any entertainment where an admission is charged. Meets for rehearsal every Tuesday evening at 8 o'clock, at Turn Verein Hall, South Spring street. Secretary's office, 118 South Main street.

Flower Festival Society-Meets first Tuesday of each month at Women's Home, 125 East Fourth street. A yearly exhibit and festival is given by the Society for the purpose of raising money to sustain the Women's Home. Mrs. A. L. Flint, President; Mrs. C. W. Blaisdell, First Vice-President; Miss M. M. Fette, Second Vice-President; Miss Fremont, Third Vice-President; Mrs. J. S. Slauson, Financial Secretary; Miss Ida Lindley, Recording Secretary.

organization_name: Women's Home of the Flower Festival Society
line: Women's Home of the Flower Festival Society-Location, 125 East Fourth street. Mrs. Post, Matron. A large, handsome building, finely finished, was recently erected by the Flower Festival Society as a Women's Home, at a cost of $14,000, where working girls can have a home at a moderate price, charged according to their ability to pay, but not to exceed $5.00 per week. There are accommodations for seventy, with thirty-six sleeping rooms.

organization_name: Women's Exchange of the Flower Festival Society
line: Women's Exchange of the Flower Festival Society-Location, 125 East Fourth street, in basement. This exchange was established by the Society, for the purpose of furnishing a place of sale for the produce of women's work, under the management of Mrs. M. F. B. Joslyn.
French Benevolent Society-Meets second Sunday of each month at corner Castelar and College. S. Vache, President; J. Sentous, Vice-President; Peter Clos, Secretary; E. Fleur, Treasurer.
German Ladies' Benevolent Association-Meets at Turn Verein Hall first Wednesday of each month, Mrs. John Milner, President; Mrs. Conrad Jacoby, Secretary.
Italian Benevolent Society-Meets second Sunday in each month at 730 North Buena Vista. Organized July, 1877. James Castruccio, President; M. Randisi, Vice-President; M. Villa, Treasurer.

organization_name: Italian Union é Frattellanza Garibaldina Society
line: Italian Union é Frattellanza Garibaldina Society-Meets first Sunday in each month at southwest corner of Macy and Old Aliso Road. Francisco Slatra, President; A. Vignolo, First Vice-President; F. Tonoi, Treasurer; F. Rangan, Secretary.
Junior Branch Christian Endeavor-Meets at First Congregational Church, southwest corner of Hill and Sixth streets, every Wednesday afternoon at 4:30 P. M.
Ladies' Aid Society-Meets at First Congregational Church, southwest corner of Hill and Sixth streets, second Tuesday in each month.

organization_name: Ladies' Auxiliary, Y. M. C. A.
line: Ladies' Auxiliary, Y. M. C. A.-Location, Y. M. C. A. Building, 209 South Broadway. Membership, 115. Meets second Monday in each month. All meetings open to visitors. Mrs. M. S. Greenwood, President; Miss Ina Baxter, Secretary; Miss Gertrude Clough, Treasurer.

Ladies' Benevolent Society-Meets every third Wednesday in each month at Pythian Castle, 118 South Spring street. Officers Mrs. C. Ducommun, President; Mrs. B. Chandler, First Vice-President; Mrs. H. L. Bath, Second Vice-President; Mrs. C. J. Wollweber, secretary; Mrs. I. W. Hellman, Treasurer.
Ladies' Missionary Society-Meets at First Congregational Church, southwest corner of Hill and Sixth streets, first Tuesday in each month.
Liquor Dealers' Union-Meets at Turn Verein Hall, first and third Friday of each month. J. M. Pearson, president; Robert Kern, Treasurer; and Henry J. A. Stuhr, Secretary.

Los Angeles Athletic Club Gymnasium and rooms in Stowell Block, 226 South Spring. Meets for practice on Monday and Thursday of each week. George H. Pike, President; R. W. Pridham, Vice-President; J. S. Thayer, Secretary; A. L. Doyle, Financial Secretary; S. B. Dewey, Treasurer.

Los Angeles Cemetery Association-Office 315 New High street. A. J. Judson, President; Victor Ponet, Superintendent; L. G. Loomis, Assistant Superintendent; A. K. Wood, Secretary; J. M. Elliott, Treasurer. Location of Cemetery, East First street, Boyle Heights.

Los Angeles County Medical Association-Organized and incorporated January 30, 1871. Meets at their Hall, rooms 20 and 21 Bryson-Bonebrake Block, northwest corner Spring and Second streets, the first and third Fridays of each month at 7:30 P. м. Officers: Dr. M. L. Moore, President; Dr. W. W. Murphy, Vice-President; Dr. H. Bert Ellis, Secretary; Dr. W. L. Dodge, Treasurer.
Los Angeles Homœopathic Society-Organized and incorporated May
Los Angeles Humane Society--Directors: H. Sinsabaugh, E. L. Baxter, C. C. Davis, Mrs. J. D. Hooper, E. W. Jones, C. L. Wills, H. T. Lee, Mrs. Harriet Wadleigh, S. Woodhead, D. Lummis, H. L. Baker.

Los Angeles Loyal Temperance Legion--Meets at I. O. G. T. Hall, 208 North Main street, every Sunday at 3 P. M.
Los Angeles Nationalist Club No. 1--Meets every Sunday afternoon, at 3 P. M., at Temperance Temple, northwest corner Temple and Broadway streets.

Los Angeles Orphans' Home Society-Location, northwest corner of Alpine and Yale streets. Organized July, 1881. Average number of orphans being cared for, 110. The object of this society shall be to take under its care orphan, half orphan and destitute children, and provide them with a home, sustenance and education.
Los Angeles Produce Exchange-Northwest corner of West First and Broadway streets.

Los Angeles Wheelmen's League Club-Location, Illinois Hall, southwest corner of Broadway and Sixth streets. Organized 1881, with a membership of six, which has since increased to forty-eight members. Purpose, social improvement. A. B. Little, President; L. D. Sale, Vice-President; H. C. F. Smith, Secretary and Treasurer; W. S. Wing, Captain. Meets first Wednesday in each month.
Los Angeles Yacht Club-Ben. E. Ward, President; George W. Williamson, Vice-President; Henry T. Hazard, Secretary and Treasurer.
Oxford League-Meets Friday evenings, at Washington Street M. E. Church (South).

organization_name: Park Church Young People's Literary Society
line: Park Church Young People's Literary Society-Meets each Friday at 7:30 P. M., at Park Congregational Church, corner of Temple and Metcalf streets. Organized December, 1886.

Plymouth Union-Organized August, 1888. Mrs. F. L. Simons, President; Mrs. W. E. Dunn, Secretary. Meets first and third Fridays of each month, at northeast corner of York and Lovelace avenue, at 2:30 P. M.

Ruskin Art Club-Meets at rooms 47 and 48, California Bank Building, at call Mrs. H. H. Boyce, President; Mrs. J. B. Owens, Treasurer; Mrs. J. F. Sartori, Secretary; Mrs. S. C. Hubbell, Chairman Executive Committee.

organization_name: Southern California Women's Christian Temperance Union
line: Southern California Women's Christian Temperance Union. Organized September, 1883. Number of members, 1050; number of auxiliaries, 75. Comprises seven counties, viz: Santa Barbara, San Diego, San Luis Obispo, Ventura, San Bernardino, Kern and Los Angeles. The Executive Committee meets quarterly every second Saturday in December, March, June and September, at Temperance Temple, corner Temple and Broadway. The Executive Committee includes State Officers, County Presidents and State Superintendents. Mrs. N. P. J. Button, president; Mrs. Mary E. Garbutt, Corresponding Secretary.
Morris Vineyard Union-Mrs. Mary Kinney, 1615 Grand avenue, President; Mrs. Amanda Bacon, 1629 Santee street, Corresponding Secretary; Mrs. L. Lamphere, 504 East Twenty-third street, treasurer.
Boyle Heights Union-Mrs. Mary A. Fisher, Station B, President; Miss Mamie Willey, 536 St. Louis street, Boyle Heights, Corresponding Secretary; Miss Lillie Rees, Station B, Treasurer.
West End Union-Mrs. J. M. Phillips, 221 Edgeware Road, President; Mrs. L. B. Croswell, 349 Edgeware Road, Corresponding Secretary; Mrs. A. Leutner, 1809 Alpine street, Treasurer.
Los Angeles Central Union-Mrs. E. M. Crow, 1012 West Seventh street, President; Mrs. N. A. Hunt, 135 South Grand avenue, Corresponding Secretary; Mrs. S. J. Oliver, 627 West Fourth street, Treasurer.
Willard Union-Mrs. Mary D. Clark, Station R, President; Miss Mabel J. Marshall, Station R, Corresponding Secretary; Miss Ella Ralston, Station D, Treasurer.
Young Women's Christian Temperance Union-Meets first and third Saturday of each month at 2:30 P. M., at First Presbyterian Church, southwest corner Broadway and Second Streets.
St. George's Society-Meets at I. O. G. T. Hall, 208 North Main street, each Friday evening at 8 o'clock. This society was organized October 15, 1886.

Turn Verein Germania-Incorporated 1871. Meets at Turner Hall, west side South Spring street, between Second and Third streets. Elections held on second Wednesdays of January and July of each year. Purposes, literary, beneficial and entertaining. J. Kuhrts, President; F. Lens, Vice-President; C. H. Voelker and C. Peterle, Secretaries.
Turners' Sisters-Meets at Turn Verein Hall, every first and third Wednesday of each month. Mrs. R. A. Fuhrberg, President; Mrs. C. Entenmann, Secretary.

Union League of Los Angeles-Organized July 26, 1888, incorporated March 22, 1889. Club rooms, southwest corner Second and Main. Officers: Fred. Eaton, President; Hervey Lindley, Vice-President; Jas. MacLachlan, Second Vice-President; John Morton, Third Vice-President; M. D. Johnson, Treasurer; B. M. Marble, Secretary; Fred. A. Duy, Assistant Secretary. Board of Directors: Fred. Eaton, Chairman; J. S. Van Doren, E. P. Johnson, J. M. Meredith, Sutherland Hutton, M. R. Higgins, C. H. Humphreys, M. D. Johnson, M. T. Owens.
Woman's Educational Club-Meets every Wednesday at 2:30 P. M., at Temperance Temple, northwest corner Temple and Broadway streets.
Woman's Suffrage Club--Meets at Temperance Temple, northwest corner North Broadway and Temple, the first and third Tuesdays of each month.

Young Men's Christian Association-Location, Y. M. C. A. Building, South Broadway near Second. Objects: Spiritual, intellectual, physical and social improvement of young men. The rooms are open to visitors from 8:30 A. M. to 9:30 P. M. Meeting for men only Sundays at 4 P. M. There is a reading room for members and visitors, with a great many daily and weekly newspapers on file. Board of Directors: E. A. Forrester, President; R. A. Crippen, Secretary; H. W. Mills, G. W. Parsons, A. E. Pomeroy. Board of Managers: A. M. Armour, Chairman; C. A. Jeffers, Recording Secretary; A. H. Voigt, Treasurer; A. B. Clapp, John Lowe, S. I. Merrill, William Nicol, F. M. Porter, E. W. Spencer, S. P. Bowen, D. M. McDonald, M. Monsen, Z. L. Parmelee, Wm. Rommell, W. M. Sheldon; General Secretary, Willard D. Ball.

organization_name: Young People's Association of the Methodist Episcopal Church
line: Young People's Association of the Methodist Episcopal Church-Boyle Heights. Meets first and third Tuesday of each month, at Methodist Episcopal Church, Boyle Heights.

organization_name: Young People's Social and Literary Society of Grace Methodist Episcopal Church
line: Young People's Social and Literary Society of Grace Methodist Episcopal Church-Location 627 East First street. Meets every Friday evening at the Church.

organization_name: Young People's Society of Christian Endeavor
line: Young People's Society of Christian Endeavor-Meets at first Congregational Church, southwest corner of Hill and Sixth streets, each Sunday evening at 6:30 o'clock.
Headquarters Department of Arizona--Geograpical limits, Territories of Arizona and New Mexico and that portion of the State of California south of the 35th parallel of north latitude.

National Guard California.

Seventh Infantry Regiment-Organized May 5, 1888. William G. Schreiber, Colonel commanding; John B. Franklin, Captain and Adjutant; L. J. Howland (Pomona) Lieutenant-Colonel; N. L. Starm (Los Angeles) Major.
Company A, Los Angeles-Franklin A. Martin, Captain. Regular meeting every Tuesday evening, Armory.
Company B, Pasadena-A. L. Hamilton, Captain. Regular meeting every Monday evening.

Company D, Ventura- , Captain. Regular meeting every Tuesday.

Company G, Anaheim-Arthur Lewis, Captain. Regular meeting every Thursday evening.
Los Angeles Infirmary.

STATE LOAN AND TRUST Co.- Incorporated January, 1889. Capital $1,000,000. Directors: P. M. Green, W. H. Perry, John Bryson Sr., George H. Bonebrake, H. J. Woollacott, L. W. Dennis, W. G. Cochran, Samuel B. Hunt, A. A. Hubbard, J. F. Towell, W. H. Crocker and O. T. Johnson.

UNIVERSITY BANK OF LOS ANGELES-Incorporated March 23, 1887. Capital stock $100,000. Number of shares of stock issued 1,000 of $100 each. Directors: R. M. Widney, S. W. Little, L. H. Titus, D. O. Miltemor, L. J. P. Morrill, C. M. Wells and C. A. Warner.

GERMAN AMERICAN SAVINGS BANK--Incorporated August 21, 1890. Capital, $100,000. Directors: E. N. McDonald, Louis Lichtenberger, W. M. Sheldon, M. N. Avery, Emanuel Eyraud, Conrad Haxen, S. W. Luitwieler, E. A. Preuss, Charles N. Flint, Dr. Joseph Kurtz and Henry Stoll.
LOS ANGELES SAVINGS BANK-Incorporated 1884. Capital stock, $100,000. Number of shares of stock issued, 1,000. Directors: I. W. Hellman, John E. Plater, Robert S. Baker and L. C. Goodwin.

organization_name: MAIN STREET SAVINGS BANK AND TRUST CO.
line: MAIN STREET SAVINGS BANK AND TRUST CO.-Incorporated October 28, 1889. Capital, $200,000. Number of shares of stock issued, 2,000. Directors: Charles Forman, George H. Pike, J. J. Schallert, I. N. Van Nuys, G. J. Griffith, J. B. Lankershim, J. H. Jones, A. Haas and I. W. Hellman.

7:30 P. M. Ladies' Mission Circle meets first Wednesday of each month at 3 P. M. Central Baptist Church. Location, northeast cor Third and Hill streets. Rev. J. S. Dill, Pastor; office at Church. Services Sundays at 11 A. M. and 7:30 P. M. Sunday school and Bible class at 9:30 A. M. Services Thursdays at 7:30 P. M. Young people's meeting Sundays at 6:30 P. M.

Christ Church (Episcopal). Location, Temperance Temple, corner of Temple and Broadway streets. Rev. Thomas W. Haskins, Rector; residence 35 California Bank Building. Services Sundays at 11 A. M. and 7:30 P. M. Sunday school at 9:45 A. M.

Christian Church. Location, Temple street near North Broadway. Rev. James B. Jones, Elder; residence 223 State street. Number of communicants 650. Services Sundays at 11 A. M. and 7:30 P. M. Sunday school at 9:45 A. M. Prayer meeting Thursdays at 7:30 P. M. Bible class Thursday evenings at 8:30 P. M.
Church of God. Location, Holiness Tabernacle, north side West Fourth street near Spring. Services Sundays at 10 A. M. Prayer meeting Thursdays at 7:30 P. M.

organization_name: Church of our Lady of Los Angeles
line: Church of our Lady of Los Angeles. Location, Main street opposite the Plaza. Rev. M. S. Liebana, Pastor. Rev. G. Genier and Rev. P. Grogan, Assistants. Pastoral residence adjoining church. Masses Sundays at 6, 8 and 10:30 A. M. Sermon in English at 8 A. M. Sermon in Spanish at 10 A. M. Vespers Sundays at 4 P. M. Sunday school after 8 o'clock mass. Devotional services at 6, 6:30 and 7 A. M. weekdays.

Church of the Ascension. Location, cor St. Louis street and Brooklyn avenue. Divine services 11 A. M and 7 P. M. Sunday school, 9:45 A. M. Holy Communion, second and fourth Sundays of each month. Litany services, Friday 7:30 P. M. Rev. D. F. Mackenzie M. A., Pastor. Residence 243 North Chicago street.

Church of the Unity. Location south side West Seventh street bet Broadway and Hill. Rev. J. S. Thomson, Pastor; residence 401 South Broadway street. Services Sundays at 11 A. M. Sunday school at 12:40 P. M.

organization_name: Los Angeles College for Young Ladies
line: Los Angeles College for Young Ladies-Location, southwest corner of Eighth and Hope streets. This is one of the young and thriving educational establishments of Los Angeles; The object of the school is to afford young ladies a thorough collegiate education in all the English branches of study, in the ancient and modern languages, in painting, drawing, etc., in elocution and in music, under the management of a board of trustees composed of many of the leading citizens of Los Angeles. The institution was formally opened on the second day of September, 1885.

Marsh's School (Miss) - Location, 1340-1342 South Hope streets. Miss Alby S. Marsh and Miss Frances C. Shoecraft, principals; Mme. Le Carpentier, Mrs. Annie Johnston, Miss Anita Rankin, Mr. William Puitti, teachers.

Occidental University of Los Angeles Location, east of the city limits between First and Second streets. This institution was formed by the Presbyterian denomination, and its aim is to secure an education that is broad and thorough. While the university will be under the care of the Presbyterians, its instruction will be evangelical rather than sectarian. J. M. Melville, president of the faculty; E. S. Field, president of board of trustees; Rev. W. S. Young, secretary; J. C. Salisbury, treasurer.
Parsons and Dennen School (Misses) - Location, 416 West Tenth street. Miss Alice K. Parsons and Miss Jeanne W. Dennen, principals. Day school for girls.

organization_name: School of the Sisters of Charity
line: School of the Sisters of Charity-Location, southwest corner Boyle and Stephenson avenues. Parents and guardians who desire to procure for their daughters and wards the advantage of a good education, in a healthy climate and a most delightful locality, will find this combined in the spacious and commodious buildings of the institution, situated on Boyle Heights and under the direction of the Sisters of Charity of St. Vincent de Paul, Sister Josephine, superioress. There is also attached to the institution a day school for young ladies.
Spanish Mission School-Location, 1039 Macy street. Mrs. L. M. Stevens, superintendent.

St. Mary's Academy-Location, 324 West Twenty-first street. A day school for young ladies under the direction of the Sisters of St. Joseph. The course of study is thorough and extensive, embracing all the branches of a solid and accomplished education.

St. Vincent's College-Location, southwest corner Grand avenue and Washington streets. This is a chartered institution in charge of and conducted by the priests of St. Vincent's parish. The course of study is classical, scientific and commercial, embracing all the branches usually taught in colleges. The college buildings are commodious and the accommodations for boarding pupils are first-class in every particular. Officers-Very Rev. A. J. Meyer, president; Rev. J. A. Linn, professor calcul., analytical geometry, Latin and English; Rev. H. Dockery, professor commercial department; Rev. J. Nichols, professor En

UNIVERSITY OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA-The University of Southern California was founded in 1879, and was formally opened for students in October, 1880. It is under control of a board of directors, elected annually by the Southern California Conference of the Methodist Episcopal Church. Education within the walls of the school, while positively Christian, is in no wise sectarian.

College of Agriculture The college was founded December 1, 1882. It is located at Ontario, San Bernardino county, on the line of the Southern Pacific Railroad, about forty miles east of Los Angeles. W.T. Randall, B. S., dean.
College of Liberal Arts-Location, west side Wesley avenue between Campus and Athena streets. Rev. W. S. Matthew, A. M., D. D., vice-president and dean. Office, 127 West First street.
College of Medicine-Location, 447 Aliso street. J. P. Widney, A. M., D. D., president and dean. Central office, 127 West First street.
College of Theology-This college was founded by Hon. Charles Maclay, for whom it was named, and is located at San Fernando, Los Angeles county. Rev. R. S. Maclay, D. D., dean.
Escondido Seminary-Located at Escondido, San Diego county. Rev. J. A. Wachob, A. M., principal.

organization_name: Board of Directors of the University
line: Board of Directors of the University-J. P. Widney, president, Los Angeles; Rev. P. F. Bresee, D. D., vice-president, Los Angeles; Rev. J. B. Green, secretary, Ontario; A. E. Pomery, treasurer, Los Angeles; David C. Cook, Los Angeles; Rev. W. A. Knighten, Los Angeles; J. Alexander Brown, Los Angeles; A. H. Naftzger, Riverside; Rev. G. W. White, A. M., San Bernardino; Rev. E. S. Chase, San Diego; Hon. J. E. McComas, Pomona.

Woodbury's Business College G. A. Hough, president; N. G. Felker, vice-president; E. C. Wilson, secretary. This college was established in 1884, and is located in the upper floor of the Stowell Block, 226 South Main street, connected by electric passenger elevator. Courses in the commercial branches, English branches, short-hand, type-writing and telegraphy. In session all the year. Twelve instructors and lecturers employed.

The Grand Commandery of Knights Templar of the State of California- Assembles annually at the Masonic Temple in the City of San Francisco on the fourth Thursday following the fourth Tuesday in April.
Cœur de Lion Commandery, No. 9-Meets third Thursday of every month at Masonic Temple.
Grand Consistory of the Ancient and Accepted Scottish Rite Meets at the Masonic Temple, in the City of San Francisco, the second Wednesday in January.

Masonic Board of Relief The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the Masters and a Committee of three of the four Lodges holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said Lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized in 1885. C. W. Blake, secretary, Court House.

organization_name: Grand Chapter of the Order of the Eastern Star of the State of California
line: The Grand Chapter of the Order of the Eastern Star of the State of California-Meets in the City of San Francisco (unless otherwise ordered) on the Monday following the second Tuesday of October in each year. The Grand Chapter was organized April 7, 1873. The first subordinate Chapter was organized May 9, 1869. There are now seventy-six subordinate Chapters under the jurisdiction of the Grand Chapter, of which one is located in the City of Los Angeles, viz: Acacia Chapter, No. 21, of the Order of Eastern Star-Meets first and third Saturday of each month at Masonic Temple.

Bar Association of Los Angeles-Organized June 5, 1888. Meets every first Saturday of each month at the Law Library. Officers: F. H. Howard, president; T. L. Winder, vice-president; R. N. Bulla, treasurer; J. W. Swanwick, recording secretary; F. L. Finlayson, financial secretary. Reading-room, 44 Court House, fourth floor.

organization_name: Board of Trade of Los Angeles
line: Board of Trade of Los Angeles-Organized March 14, 1883. Number of members, 78. Office, northwest corner of Broadway and West First street. Objects: For the equal protection of the interests of its members; to prevent settlements without full investigation; the attachment by any member of this board of any common debtor or debtors shall be for the mutual benefit of all board members having claims against said debtor or debtors. Officers: R. H. Howell, president; A. Jacoby, vice-president; Gregory Perkins Jr., secretary; L. N. Breed, treasurer; Graff & Latham, attorneys.
British Benevolent Society-Meets first Monday in each month at 218 North Main street, Lanfranco Building.

California Club-Organized 1888. Location, northwest corner Broadway and First streets. Officers: C. Cabot, president; M. S. Severance, vice-president; Gratz K. Brown, secretary.

Catholic Ladies' Aid Society-Mrs. J. Kenealy, president; Mrs. George Kerckhoff, treasurer; Mrs. M. C. Holmes, secretary; Mrs. D. F. Donegan, first vice-president; Mrs. Dr. Reed, second vice-president; Mrs. Joseph Mesmer, third vice-president. Meets at 1154 North Main street.

organization_name: Chamber of Commerce of Los Angeles
line: Chamber of Commerce of Los Angeles-Organized October 15, 1888. Present membership, 450. Location, 137 South Main street, Mott Block. Meetings second Wednesday of each month at 7:30 P. M. Its objects are to foster and encourage commerce; to stimulate home manufactures; to induce immigration and the subdivision, settlement and cultivation of our lands; to assist in the development of the material resources of the region, and generally to promote the business interests of Los Angeles city and county and the country tributary thereto. D. Freeman, president; C. D. Willard, secretary; L. N. Breed, treasurer.

Its objects are to foster and encourage commerce; to stimulate home manufactures; to induce immigration and the subdivision, settlement and cultivation of our lands; to assist in the development of the material resources of the region, and generally to promote the business interests of Los Angeles city and and county and the country tributary thereto. Ellis Club-Organized April 3, 1888, for the study of music for Male voices, and also for the culture and develop ment of a refined musical taste in its members. Active members (singing), 60; auxiliary members (substitutes), 12; associate members (subscribing), 200. Supported by an annual subscription of $10 from the associate members. Gives four concerts each year, to each of which the associate members receive four tickets. It is a strictly private organization, the membership being elective, and no tickets sold. Does not take part in any entertainment where an admission is charged. Meets for rehearsal every Tuesday evening.

organization_name: Women's Home of the Flower Festival Society
line: Women's Home of the Flower Festival Society Location, 125 E. Fourth street. Mrs. A. Stout, matron. A large, handsome building, finely finished, was erected by the Flower Festival Society as a Women's Home, at a cost of $14,000, where working girls can have a home at a moderate price, charged according to their ability to pay, but not to exceed $5.00 per week. There are accommodations for seventy, with thirty sleeping rooms.

The object of this society shall be to take under its care orphan, half orphan and destitute children, and provide them with a home, sustenance and education. Any person may be an active member of this society by the annual payment of $2; an honorary member by the payment of $10; and a life member by the payment of $100 at one time. Mrs. D. G. Young Men's Christian Association- Location, Y. M. C. A. Building. Objects: Spiritual, intellectual, physical and social improvement of young men. The rooms are open to visitors from 8:30 A. M. to 9:30 P. M. Meetings for men only, Sundays at 4 P. M. There is a reading-room for members and visitors with a great many daily and weekly newspapers on file. organization_name: Grand Chapter of the Order of the Eastern Star of the State of California
line: The Grand Chapter of the Order of the Eastern Star of the State of California-Meets in the city of San Francisco (unless otherwise ordered) on the Monday following the second Tuesday of October in each year. The Grand Chapter was organized April 7, 1873. The first subordinate Chapter was organized May 9, 1869. There are now seventy-six subordinate Chapters under the jurisdiction of the Grand Chapter, of which one is located in the city of Los Angeles, viz.: Acacia Chapter, No. 21, of the Order of Eastern Star- Meets first and third Saturday of each month at Masonic Temple. Los Angeles Board of Trade- Organized March 14, 1883. Number of members, 87; rooms third floor Bullard Block. Objects: For the equal protection of the interests of its members, to prevent settlement without full investigation, the attachment by any member of this board of any common debtor or debtors shall be for the mutual benefit of all board members having claims against said debtor or debtors. Officers: A. Jacoby, president; R. P. Bishop, vice-president; Gregory Perkins, Jr., secretary; E. P. Johnson, treasurer; Graff & Latham, attorneys.

Los Angeles Chamber of Commerce Organized October 15, 1888. Present membership, $50. Location, SE. cor. S. Broadway and W. Fourth. Meetings of board of directors every Wednesday afternoon at 3:15. Its objects are to foster and encourage commerce; to stimulate home manufactures; to induce immigration and the subdivision, settlement and cultivation of our lands; to assist in the development of the material resources of the region, and generally to promote the business interests of Los Angeles city and county and the country tributary thereto. W. C. Patterson, president; C. D. Willard, secretary; Frank Wiggins, superintendent.

Los Angeles Humane Society Officers: Dr. W. Lindley, president; Miss E. Mills, secretary; D. Hutchins, special officer. Meets at call of president. Objects: The prevention of cruelty to children and animals, and the punishment of offenders against the law made for their protection. The chief aim of the society is to create and foster such a public sentiment as shall render such offenses rare. To this end the hearty cooperation of all good citizens is sought. Under our laws the work is done under two organizations, one for the prevention of cruelty to children and the other for the prevention of cruelty to animals. The annual dues of each society are $2.50. Life membership in the S. P. C. A., $50. In the S. P. C. C., contributing members, $1 per year; junior members (children under 15 years), 50 cents; honorary life membership, $100.

Los Angeles Chamber of Commerce. Organized October 18, 1888. Present membership, 850: Location, se cor. S. Broadway and W. Fourth. Meetings of board of directors every Wednesday afternoon at 3:15. Its objects are to foster and encourage commerce: to stimulate home manufactures: to induce immigration and the subdivision: settlement and cultivation of our lands: to assist in the development of the material resources of the region, and generally to promote the business interests of Los Angeles, city, and county and the country tributary thereto. Object: The prevention of cruelty to children and animals, and the punishment of offenders against the law made for their protection: the chief aim of the society is to create and foster such a public sentiment as shall render such offenses rare: to this end the hearty co-operation of all good citizens is sought; under our laws the work is done under two organizations, one for the prevention of cruelty to children and the other for the prevention of cruelty to animals: the annual dues of each society are $2.50; life membership in the S. P. C. A. $50: in the S. P. C. C. contributing members, $1 a year. junior members (children under 15 years), 50c; honorary life membership, $100.

organization_name: Grand Lodge of Free and Accepted Masons of the State of California
line: MASONIC. Grand Lodge of Free and Accepted Masons of the State of California, assembles annually at the Masonic Temple in the city of San Francisco, on the 2nd Tuesday in October. This body was organized in the year 1850, and there are now in existence, under its jurisdiction, two hundred and sixty-two subordinate lodges, of which six are in the city of Los Angeles, as follows:
Hollenbeck Lodge No. 319, meets second Monday in each month at cor. N. Chicago and E. 1st, Boyle Heights; J. Will Dick, secy.

Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each six lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized 1885, Oscar Lawler, secy.

This body was organized in the year 1860, and there are now in existence under its jurisdiction thirteen subordinate councils, of which one is held in the City of Los Angeles.

Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of six lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized 1855, Oscar Lawler, sec.

Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of six lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized 1855. Dr. JM Dunsmoor sec.

Fraternal Brotherhood, The Supreme Lodge of the Fraternal Brotherhood was organized in Los Angeles and the first lodge instituted on March 17, 1896. The headquarters are in Los Angeles, and the Order of the Fraternal Brotherhood is a home institution. The membership, March, 1903, was 20,000. It has in good standing 258 lodges. Nia assembles annually on Monday in the previous to the third Tuesday April, at the Masonic Temple in the City of San Francisco. This body was organized in the year 1860, and there are now in existence under its jurisdiction thirteen subordinate Councils, of which one is held in the City of Los Angeles.

organization_name: Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles
line: Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of ten lodges holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized 1855. Dr. JM Dunsmoor, sec.

Masonic Board of Relief The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of ten lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized 1855, Dr JM Dunsmoor, sec.

Masonic Board of Relief - The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given to the needy of the order. Organized 1855, Dr JM Dunsmoor, sec.

organization_name: Supreme Lodge of the Fraternal Brotherhood
line: The Supreme Lodge of the Fraternal Brotherhood was organized in Los Angeles and the first lodge instituted on March 17, 1896. The headquarters are in Los Angeles, and the Order of the Fraternal Brotherhood is a home Institution. The membership, Jan 1. 1907, was 32,008. It has in good standing 386 lodges. Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being the sole recipients of its charities. Y M C A. Young Men's Christian Association, location 715-721 S Hope; object: Spiritual, intellectual, physical and social improvement of young men: the rooms are open to visitors from 8:30 a m to 10 p m: meetings for men only Sunday at 3 p m. A modern hotel is provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rates; there is a reading room for members and visitors with a great many daily and weekly newspapers on file, gymnasium, baths, entertainments, evening educational classes, employment bureau, etc. are maintained for the members of whom there are about 3000: Arthur Letts, pres; D E Luther, general sec.

Y W C A. Young Women's Christian Association, 251 S Hill. Object: The physical, social, intellectual and spiritual development of young women. The rooms are open from 3 a m to 9 p m: meetings for women Sundays at 4 p m. reading room supplied with daily papers and current periodicals; lunch room open to women from 11 a m to 2 p m; educational classes, entertainments, etc.; membership 6129; Mrs Frank A Dewey, pres; Miss Cora L Tatham, general sec.

Masonic Board of Relief - The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. A modern hotel is provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rates; there is a reading room for members and visitors with a great many daily and weekly newspapers on file, gymnasium, baths, entertainments, evening educational classes, employment bureau, etc, are maintained for the members of whom there are about 4000; Arthur Letts, pres; D E Luther, general sec.

organization_name: Grand Lodge of Free and Accepted Masons of the State of California

Masonic Board of Relief - The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being the only recipients of its charities. Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being the only recipients of its charities. organization_name: Fraternal Brotherhood The Supreme Lodge of the Fraternal Brotherhood
line: Fraternal Brotherhood The Supreme Lodge of the Fraternal Brotherhood was organized in Los Angeles and the first lodge instituted on March 17, 1896. The headquarters are in Los Angeles, and the Order of the Fraternal Brotherhood is a home institution. The membership, Jan. 1, 1913, was 43,000. It has in good standing 700 lodges. Masonic Board of Relief The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of three members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given the needy of the order. Organized 1881, Dr J M Dunsmoor sec, 506 Stimson Bldg.

Masonic Employment Bureau, 505 Stimson Bldg. Fred T Purdy mgr.

organization_name: Board of Relief of Los Angeles
line: The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. organization_name: WCTU Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California
line: WCTU Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California, organized September 1883; comprises eleven counties, viz: Santa Luis Obispo, Santa Barbara, San Diego Ventura, San Bernardino, Kern, Los Angeles, Riverside, Imperial and Orange. The executive committee meets quarterly the first Saturday in December, March, June and September at Temperance Temple. The executive committee includes state officers county presidents and state superintendents Mrs Stella B Irvine pres, Mrs Hester Griffith v-pres, Mary Stewart, Pasadena, treas Mrs Alice P Woertendyke cor sec, Mrs Hattie C Young. Long Beach, rec sec; state headquarters 301 N Broadway. Los Angeles County WCTU headquarters, 301 N Broadway, Mrs Julia D Phelps pres, 3140 San Marino. Temperance Temple Financial Board. Mrs L E Meserve pres, Mrs E A Oakley, Mrs Lizzie Mills directors, Mrs Mary E Garbutt treas, Miss Annie Strauss sec, meets 1st Tuesday of each month.

YMCA Young Men's Christian Association, location 715-733 S Hope st; object, spiritual, intellectual, physical and social improvement of men and boys; rooms are open to visitors from 8:30 am to 10 pm. Meetings for men only Sundays at 3 pm. Dormitory apartments are provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rates; also public cafeteria open continuously to men and women. The reading room for members is supplied with many periodicals, daily, weekly and monthly; gymnasium, baths, entertainments, evening educational and Bible classes, vocational bureau, etc., are maintained for the members, of whom there are approximately 5000. A B Cass pres. E C Lyon chairman, committee of management, DE Luther gen sec. YMCA Athletic Field and Clubhouse, 2834 Stephenson av, has an 8-acre athletic field with clubhouse in which there are dormitories accommodating 70 men. WA Stilwell sec, mgr and athletic director.

organization_name: Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California
line: Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California, organized September, 1883; comprises eleven counties, viz: San Luis Obispo, Santa Barbara, San Diego, Ventura, San Bernardino, Kern, Los Angeles, Riverside, Imperial and Crange. The executive committee meets quarterly the first Saturday in December, March, June and September at Temperance Temple. The executive committee includes state officers, county presidents and state superintendents; Mrs Stella B Irvine pres, Mrs Hester Guifith v-pres, Mary Stewart, Pasadena, treas, Mrs Alice P Woertendyke cor sec, Mrs Hattie C Young. Long Beach, rec sec; state headquarters 301 N Broadway, Los Angeles County W CTU headquarters. 301 N Broadway. Temperance Temple Financial Board, Mrs LE Meserve pres, Miss Mary Stewart, Mrs Hattie Young and Mrs Stella B Irvine directors, Mrs Mary E Garbutt treas; Mrs FR Houghan sec; meets 1st Tuesday of each month.

Young Men's Christian Association, location 715-733 S Hope st; object, spiritual, intellectual, physical and social improvement of men and boys; rooms are open to visitors from 8:30 am to 10 pm. Dormitory apartments are provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rates; also public cafeteria open continuously to men and women. The reading room for members is supplied with many periodicals, daily, weekly and monthly; gymnasium, baths, entertainments, evening educational and Bible classes, vocational bureau, etc. are maintained for the members of whom there are approximately 5000. A B Cass pres, Dr Se J Lund chairman, committee of management, DE Luther gen sec.

organization_name: WCTU Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California
line: WCTU Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California, organized September 1883; comprises eleven counties, viz: San Ventura, San Bernardino, Kern, Los Angeles Obispo, Santa Barbara, San Diego, geles, Riverside, Imperial and Orange. The executive committee meets quarterly the first Saturday in December, March, June and September at Temperance Temple. The executive committee includes state officers, county presidents and state superintendents; Mrs Stella B Irvine pres. Mrs Eva C Wheeler v-pres, Mary Stewart, Pasadena, treas, Mrs Kara S Root cor sec, Mrs Tettie Loung Long Beach, rec sec; state headquarters 301 N Broadway, Los Angeles County WCTU headquarters, 301 N Broadway. Temperance Temple Board of Trustees, Mrs LE Meserve pres, Mrs Kara S Root, Mrs Hattie Young and. Mrs Stella B Irvine directors, Mrs Mary E Garbutt treas; Mrs Alice P Woertendyke sec: meets 1st Tuesday of each month.

YMCA Young Men's Christian Association, location 715-733 S Hope st; object, spiritual intellectual, physical and social improvement of men and boys; rooms are open to visitors from 8:30 am to 10 pm. Dormitory apartments are provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rates; also public cafeteria open continuously to men and women. The reading room for members is supplied with many periodicals, daily, weekly and monthly: gymnasium, baths, entertainments, evening educational and Bible classes, vocational bureau, etc, are maintained for the members of whom there are approximately 5000. A B Cass pres, Dr Geo J Lund chairman, committee of management, DE Luther gen sec.

line: Masonic Grand Lodge of Free and Accepted Masons of the state of California, assembles in the City of San Francisco on the second Tuesday of October. This body was organized in the year 1850, and there are now in existence under its jurisdiction twenty-eight subordinate lodges in the City of Los Angeles as follows, the date of the stated meeting being given:
Masonic Employment Bureau, 511 Stimson bldg, Fred T Purdy mgr.

Masonic Board of Relief The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given the needy of the order. Organized 1881. Dr J M Dunsmoor sec. 513 Stimson bldg.

organization_name: Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California
line: W. C. T. U. MISCELLANEOUS INFORMATION Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California, organized September 1883, comprises eleven counties, viz: San Luis Obispo, Santa Barbara, San Diego, Ventura, San Bernardino, Kern, Los Angeles, Riverside, Imperial, Inyo and Orange. The executive committee meets quarterly in December, March, June and September at Temperance Temple. The executive committee includes state officers, county presidents and state superintendents; Mrs Stella B Irvine pres, Mrs Eva C Wheeler v-pres, Mrs Belle Mulford treas, Gabrella T Stickney corr sec, Mrs Hattie C Young Long Beach rec sec; state headquarters 301 N Broad-way, Temperance Temple Board of Trustees, Mrs L E Meserve pres, Mrs Stella B Irvine v-pres, Mrs Hattie C Young sec, Mrs Mary E Garbutt treas; Mrs Gabrella T Stickney corr sec, meets 1st Tuesday of each month. Y. M. C. Α. Young Men's Christian Association location 715-733 S Hope st; object, spiritual intellectual, physical and social improvement of men and boys; rooms are open to visitors from 8:30 am to 10 pm. Dormitory apartments are provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rates; also public cafeteria open continuously to men and women. The reading room for members is supplied with many periodicals, daily, weekly and monthly; gymnasium, baths, entertainments, evening educational and Bible classes, vocational bureau, etc, are maintained for the members of whom there are approximately 5000. A B Cass pres. Dr Geo J Lund chairman, committee of management, D E Luther gen sec. YMCA Athletic Field and Clubhouse, 2834 Stephenson av, has an 8-acre athletic field with clubhouse in which there are dormitories accommodating 70 men. W A Stilwell sec, mgr and athletic director. line: Grand Lodge of Free and Accepted Masons of the State of California, assembles in the City of San Francisco on the second Tuesday of October. Masonic Employment Bureau. 511 Stimson bldg. Fred T Purdy mgr.

Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to, but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons, their widows and orphans, being alone recipients of its charities. Money refunded is again given the needy of the order. Organized 1881. Dr JM Dunsmoor sec, 513 Stimson bldg.

Masonic Board of Relief-The Board of Relief of Los Angeles is composed of the masters and a committee of two members of each of the lodges, holding concurrent jurisdiction in Los Angeles. Said lodges contribute to but draw nothing from the funds of the board, sojourning Master Masons their widows and orphans being alone recipients of its charities. PUBLIC LIBRARY The library occupies the four upper floors of the Metropolitan Building, 5th and Broadway; open 9 am to 9 pm every day in the year except New Year's Day, Washington's Birthday, Memorial Day, Fourth of July, Labor Day, Thanksgiving and Christmas; Sundays from 1 to 9, Periodical Department only. There are on file in the reading rooms 2900 periodicals; 1725 newspapers. The Library contains 489,507 volumes. Privileges open to all. Branches are also maintained in all sections of the city: also 228 deposit stations, of which 81 are Public School stations. The Library also maintains a Library School Board of Directors of Los Angeles Public Library O E Monnéte, pres: Rev Francis J Conaty, Frank H Pettingell, Mrs Katherine G Smith, Mrs Frances H Zahn, members of the Board; Everett R Perry, librarian. Meetings of the Board 2d working day and 3d Wednesday of each month at 3:30 pm in the Library

organization_name: Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California
line: WCTU Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California. Organised September, 1883. Comprises eleven counties, viz: San Luis Obispo, Santa Barbara, San Diego, Ventura, San Bernardino, Kern, Los Angeles, Riverside, Imperial, Inyo and Orange. The executive committee meets quarterly in December, March, June and September, at Temperance Temple. The executive committee includes state officers, county presidents and state superintendents. Headquarters, 301 N Bway, Temperance Temple

YMCA Young Men's Christian Association, location 715-723 S Hope. Object, spiritual, intellectual, physical and social improvement of men and boys; rooms are open to visitors from 8 a m to 10 p m. Dormitory apartments are provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rate; also public cafeteria open continuously to men and women. The reading room for members is supplied with many periodicals, daily, weekly and monthly; gymnasium, fresh water plunge, handball courts, baths, entertainments, day and evening educational classes and Bible classes, vocational bureau, etc, are maintained for the members of whom there are approximately 6,000. line: Woman's Christian Temperance Union of Southern California. Organized September, 1883. Comprises eleven counties, viz: San Luis Obispo, Santa Barbara, San Diego, Ventura, San Bernardino, Kern, Los Angeles, Riverside, Imperial, Inyo and Orange. The executive committee meets quarterly in December, March, June and September, at Temperance Temple. The executive committee includes state officers, county presidents and state superintendents. Headquarters, 301 N Bway. Temperance Temple

organization_name: Young Men's Christian Association of California (State Executive Committee)
line: Young Men's Christian Association, location, 715-723 8 Hope. Object, spiritual, intellectual, physical and social improvement of men and boys; rooms are open to visitors from 8 a m to 10 p m. Dormitory apartments are provided for members where good rooms may be secured at reasonable rate: also public cafeteria open continuously to men and women, The reading room for members is supplied with many periodicals, daily, weekly and monthly; gymnasium, fresh water plunge, handball courts, baths, entertainments, day and evening vocational classes and Bible classes, vocational bureau, etc. are maintained for the members of whom there are approximately 6,000. F E Eckhart, Pres, Harry F Henderson General Secretary. Hollywood Branch, Selma av cor Hudson, Gymnasiums, swimming pool, club room and outdoor tennis courts. Fred L Dye Executive sec. Twenty-eighth Street Branch (colored), T A Greene Executive Sec, cor Paloma and E 28th University of Southern California Branch, Glen E Turner Executive Secretary, 3623 University av University of California Southern Branch, Guy C Harris Executive Secretary, 803 Heliotrope dr Hollenbeck Heights Branch, 2834 Whittier Blvd. has an 8-acre athletic field with club house in which are dormitories accommodating 70 men; tennis courts, two basketball fields. A H Maxwell Executive sec. Occidental College Branch, Campus rd (San Pedro) Young Men's Christian Association (Army and Navy).
